news.title: The World Cup champions will receive championship rings for the first time in history
news.lede: The 2026 World Cup final between Sapin and Argentina will have a historic and unprecedented finale in the history of football. 'FIFA reports that "the champions of the FIFA World Cup will receive custom championship rings, bringing one of the most recognizable American sports traditions to world football".' "Besides the iconic trophy and the prestigious gold medals, the winning team will receive, for the first time in a FIFA competition, an additional recognition: personalized championship rings. In this way, FIFA is bringing into football one of the most deeply rooted traditions in American sports," FIFA said in a statement.

news.cat: THE LIBRARY OF ECONOMICS AND LIBERTY
news.title: A World Cup Final Through Austrian Eyes
news.lede: By Giacomo Balbinotto Neto, Jul 17 2026. Imagine that history made one small exception. For one evening only, time stopped separating generations. High above the noise of a packed World Cup final game, in a quiet corner of the grandstand untouched by television cameras, eight elderly gentlemen quietly took their seats.

news.title: Which World Cup teams were most surprising and most disappointing?
news.lede: The 2026 FIFA World Cup has delivered everything fans could have hoped for: drama, upsets, breakout stars, and a few stunning disappointments. While powerhouses like Spain, Argentina, France, and England ultimately reached the semifinals, the road there was filled with performances that nobody saw coming. That brings us to today’s Question of the Day: Which World Cup team surprised you the most, and which team was the biggest disappointment? There are plenty of worthy candidates on both sides of the debate.

news.title: FIFA is giving World Cup winners championship rings. Yes, fans can get one too
news.lede: Football purists may want to look away now. FIFA is about to give the beautiful game a very American makeover, and the prize for winning the 2026 World Cup could include something far flashier than a medal.Championship rings are coming to football.With the final between Spain and Argentina set to take place at MetLife Stadium, FIFA is embracing the tournament’s North American setting with a tradition more commonly associated with the Super Bowl: Enormous, outrageously expensive jewellery designed to announce, quite loudly, that you won. . FIFA World Cup 2026 final: How Argentina's Spanish colonial past and now Messi connect the nations.And these are not the sort of rings you pick up at a stadium gift shop. FIFA is producing just 2,026 of them, with 30 reserved for members of the winning team.

news.title: The World Cup Third-Place Play-Off: Is It the Weirdest Game in Football?
news.lede: France will play England in the 2026 World Cup third-place play-off on Saturday. We look back on what some see as a bit of an odd fixture at the tournament. After 102 games, there are just two remaining at the 2026 World Cup. One is the biggest game in football, the World Cup final.

news.title: 2026 World Cup Predictions
news.lede: At the start of this exceptional World Cup, our model had Argentina and Spain as co-favorites, with France and England as the 3rd- and 4th-most likely teams to take home the trophy. I understand the skepticism about Argentina. You could argue that all three of their KO-round wins have been dodgy in some fashion. I thought they played an excellent game today, by contrast, though I’m not quite sure whether their conservative play in the first half served any greater purpose.

news.title: Is men’s college soccer a viable path to the World Cup? Here’s how it’s changing
news.lede: The men's college game is hardly dead, but it is not necessarily a destination for top domestic talent.

news.title: The New York hotel staff who welcomed World Cup fans – then became them
news.lede: Twenty-four hotel employees from New York and New Jersey were offered the chance to attend England's win over Panama at MetLife Stadium.
